intitule: Exercise 1: Reading Comprehension - News Report on Jobs

enonce.texte:
Read the following radio news report about jobs and answer the questions.

RADIO NEWS: JOBS IN CAMEROON

By Radio Cameroon News Desk

Good evening, listeners! Welcome to our special report on jobs and professions in Cameroon.

In recent years, many young Cameroonians have been looking for jobs in different sectors. The most popular professions are teaching, nursing, engineering, and information technology. According to the Ministry of Labour, there is a high demand for skilled workers in these fields.

However, many young people say they are worried about job opportunities. Some students prefer to work in the private sector, while others are interested in public service. There are also those who choose to become entrepreneurs and start their own businesses.

Experts encourage young people to develop practical skills. They say that education and training are very important. Many companies are now looking for people who can speak English and French, as well as people with computer skills.

We asked some young people about their opinions on jobs. Here are their responses:

"I think teaching is a noble profession. I love working with children," said Marie, 18.

"I believe nursing is very rewarding. It allows me to help people," said Jean, 19.

"In my opinion, technology is the future. I want to be a computer engineer," said Paul, 17.

questions.texte: What are the most popular professions mentioned in the report?
questions.reponse: The most popular professions mentioned are teaching, nursing, engineering, and information technology.

questions.texte: List three sectors where young Cameroonians are looking for jobs.
questions.reponse: Three sectors where young Cameroonians are looking for jobs are: 1) Teaching, 2) Nursing, 3) Engineering (or Information Technology).

questions.texte: What advice do experts give to young people regarding jobs?
questions.reponse: Experts advise young people to develop practical skills and to focus on education and training. They also mention that speaking English and French and having computer skills are important for getting jobs.

questions.texte: Analyze the different opinions expressed by Marie, Jean, and Paul about their preferred professions.
questions.reponse: Marie thinks teaching is a noble profession because she loves working with children. Jean believes nursing is rewarding because it allows him to help people. Paul thinks technology is the future and wants to become a computer engineer. Each person has a different motivation: Marie is motivated by passion, Jean by helping others, and Paul by future opportunities.

enonce.texte:
Read the statements about different professions. Write your opinion using the expressions provided.

Useful expressions for expressing opinions:

  • I think... (Je pense...)
  • In my opinion... (À mon avis...)
  • I believe... (Je crois...)
  • I feel... (Je ressens...)
  • I am interested in... (Je suis intéressé par...)
  • I would like to... (Je voudrais...)

questions.texte:
"I think teaching is a very important job."
Do you agree? Write your opinion.
questions.reponse:
Example answers:

  • "Yes, I agree. I think teaching is very important because teachers shape the future of our country."
  • "In my opinion, teaching is one of the most noble professions."
  • "I believe teaching is important, but it is also very challenging."

questions.texte:
"Nursing is the best profession for people who want to help others."
Express your opinion.
questions.reponse:
Example answers:

  • "I agree. In my opinion, nursing is a very rewarding profession because you help people every day."
  • "I think there are many other professions that also help people, like teaching and social work."
  • "I feel nursing is a great profession, but it requires a lot of patience and dedication."

questions.texte:
"Doctors are more important than farmers."
What do you think? Give your opinion.
questions.reponse:
Example answers:

  • "I disagree. I think both doctors and farmers are equally important. Farmers provide food, and doctors keep us healthy."
  • "In my opinion, every profession is important. We cannot say one is more important than another."
  • "I believe doctors and farmers are both essential for our society. They serve different but equally important roles."

enonce.texte:
Write a dialogue (8-10 exchanges) between two friends discussing their job preferences and opinions about different professions.

Your dialogue should include:

  • A greeting and introduction
  • Each person's preferred profession
  • Reasons for their choice
  • At least 3 opinion expressions (I think, in my opinion, I believe, etc.)
  • A conclusion

questions.texte: Write a dialogue about job preferences.
questions.reponse:
Example Dialogue:

Jean: "Hi Marie! What job do you want to do in the future?"

Marie: "Hi Jean! I believe I want to become a nurse. I like helping people."

Jean: "That's interesting. In my opinion, nursing is a very rewarding profession."

Marie: "Yes, I think so too. What about you? What do you want to do?"

Jean: "I think I want to be an engineer. I love building things and solving problems."

Marie: "Engineering is a good job. I feel it is a profession with many opportunities."

Jean: "Yes, I believe technology is the future. That's why I am interested in engineering."

Marie: "I am happy for you. I hope we both achieve our dreams!"

Jean: "Thank you, Marie. I hope so too. Let's work hard to make our dreams come true."

enonce.texte:
Read the following text and answer the questions. It is based on a radio interview about job preferences.

RADIO INTERVIEW: WHAT DO YOUNG PEOPLE WANT TO DO?

Interviewer: "Today we are talking to three young people about their job preferences. Let's start with David. David, what job do you want to do?"

David: "I want to be a pilot. I love travelling and I think it is an exciting job."

Interviewer: "That's great! What about you, Sarah?"

Sarah: "In my opinion, I think I want to become a lawyer. I like arguing and defending people's rights."

Interviewer: "Law is a very interesting profession. And you, Charles?"

Charles: "I believe I want to be a chef. I love cooking and creating new dishes."

Interviewer: "Thank you all for sharing your preferences. What advice would you give to other young people?"

David: "Follow your dreams and never give up."

Sarah: "Study hard and believe in yourself."

Charles: "Do what you love and you will never work a day in your life."

questions.texte: What job does David want to do and why?
questions.reponse: David wants to be a pilot because he loves travelling and thinks it is an exciting job.

questions.texte: What is Sarah's preferred profession and why?
questions.reponse: Sarah wants to become a lawyer because she likes arguing and defending people's rights.

questions.texte: What advice does Charles give to other young people?
questions.reponse: Charles advises other young people to "Do what you love and you will never work a day in your life."

questions.texte: Compare the three different job preferences. What do they have in common?
questions.reponse: All three young people are passionate about their chosen professions. David is excited about travelling, Sarah is passionate about justice, and Charles loves creativity in cooking. They all believe in following their dreams and are motivated by personal interests rather than just money.

enonce.texte:
Write a short paragraph (80-100 words) about your preferred profession and why you like it.

Your paragraph should include:

  • The name of the profession you prefer
  • At least 3 expressions of opinion (I think, in my opinion, I believe, etc.)
  • Reasons for your preference
  • What you need to do to achieve this profession

questions.texte: Write a paragraph about your preferred profession.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

MY DREAM JOB

I believe that I want to become a teacher. In my opinion, teaching is one of the most important professions because teachers shape the future of our country. I think I would enjoy working with children and helping them learn new things.

I feel that teaching is a very rewarding job because you see your students grow and succeed. I am interested in education because I love learning and sharing knowledge.

To achieve my dream, I think I need to study hard and get a good education. I also need to be patient and dedicated. I believe that with hard work, I can become a great teacher.

enonce.texte: Match the professions with the adjectives that best describe them.

questions.texte:
Match:
Professions:
1. Teacher
2. Doctor
3. Nurse
4. Engineer
5. Lawyer
6. Chef
7. Pilot
8. Artist

Adjectives:
A. Creative
B. Caring
C. Compassionate
D. Patient
E. Adventurous
F. Analytical
G. Persuasive
H. Skilled

Match each profession with the most appropriate adjective.
questions.reponse:
1-D (Teacher - Patient), 2-C (Doctor - Compassionate), 3-B (Nurse - Caring), 4-F (Engineer - Analytical), 5-G (Lawyer - Persuasive), 6-H (Chef - Skilled), 7-E (Pilot - Adventurous), 8-A (Artist - Creative).

Explication: Ces adjectifs décrivent les qualités souvent associées à chaque profession. Un enseignant doit être patient, un docteur compatissant, un infirmier attentionné, un ingénieur analytique, un avocat persuasif, un chef qualifié, un pilote aventureux et un artiste créatif.

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences with appropriate opinion expressions from the box.

Expressions: I think, In my opinion, I believe, I feel, I am interested in, I would like to

questions.texte:
1. ______ (I think / I believe) that being a doctor is a very noble profession.
2. ______ (In my opinion / I feel), teaching is more rewarding than many other jobs.
3. ______ (I am interested in / I think) becoming a chef because I love cooking.
4. ______ (I would like to / I feel) work as an engineer in the future.
5. ______ (I believe / I think) that everyone should choose a job they love.
6. ______ (In my opinion / I am interested in), nursing is a profession that requires a lot of patience.
questions.reponse:
1. I think (or I believe)
2. In my opinion (or I feel)
3. I am interested in
4. I would like to
5. I believe (or I think)
6. In my opinion

intitule: Exercise 8: Creative Writing - Interviewing a Professional

enonce.texte:
Imagine you are interviewing a professional (teacher, doctor, engineer, etc.). Write a short interview (10-12 exchanges) about their job.

Your interview should include:

  • A greeting and introduction
  • Questions about the profession (What do you do? Why did you choose this job? Do you like it?)
  • Opinions from the professional
  • A conclusion

questions.texte: Write an interview with a professional.
questions.reponse:
Example Interview:

Interviewer: "Good morning, Mr. Tchoffo. Thank you for accepting this interview."

Mr. Tchoffo: "Good morning. It's my pleasure."

Interviewer: "What is your profession?"

Mr. Tchoffo: "I am a teacher. I teach English at a secondary school."

Interviewer: "Why did you choose this profession?"

Mr. Tchoffo: "I believe that teaching is a very important job. I think teachers can change lives."

Interviewer: "Do you like your job?"

Mr. Tchoffo: "Yes, I love my job. In my opinion, it is very rewarding to see students succeed."

Interviewer: "What advice would you give to young people?"

Mr. Tchoffo: "I believe young people should choose a profession they are passionate about. Hard work is the key to success."

Interviewer: "Thank you for your time."

Mr. Tchoffo: "You're welcome. Thank you for this interview."

enonce.texte:
You are a career counsellor. A student comes to you for advice about job possibilities. Write a dialogue (8-10 exchanges) where you give advice.

Your dialogue should include:

  • Questions about the student's interests
  • Opinions and advice
  • Suggestions for different professions
  • Encouragement

questions.texte: Write a dialogue between a career counsellor and a student.
questions.reponse:
Example Dialogue:

Counsellor: "Good morning. How can I help you?"

Student: "Good morning. I need advice about choosing a profession."

Counsellor: "What subjects do you enjoy at school?"

Student: "I think I am good at science and mathematics."

Counsellor: "In my opinion, you could become an engineer or a doctor. These professions require science and mathematics."

Student: "I am interested in medicine. I believe I want to become a doctor."

Counsellor: "That's a great choice! I think you need to study hard and focus on your science subjects."

Student: "What about other options?"

Counsellor: "You could also consider nursing or pharmacy. I feel there are many opportunities in the medical field."

Student: "Thank you for your advice."

Counsellor: "You're welcome. I believe you will succeed if you work hard."

intitule: Exercise 10: Comprehensive Writing - My Dream Job

enonce.texte:
Write a comprehensive essay (150-180 words) about your dream job.

Your essay should include:

  • An introduction presenting the profession
  • Why you chose this profession (reasons and motivations)
  • What you need to do to achieve this goal
  • Your opinion about the profession
  • At least 5 expressions of opinion (I think, in my opinion, I believe, etc.)
  • A conclusion

questions.texte: Write an essay about your dream job.
questions.reponse:
Example Essay:

MY DREAM JOB: BECOMING A TEACHER

I believe that my dream job is to become a teacher. In my opinion, teaching is one of the most important professions in the world because teachers shape the future of our society.

I think I would be a good teacher because I love working with children and helping them learn. When I was young, my teacher inspired me to love learning, and I feel that I can do the same for others. I am interested in education because it allows me to share knowledge and make a difference.

To achieve my dream, I believe I need to study hard and get a good education. I need to be patient, dedicated, and passionate about my work. I also need to continue learning throughout my career.

In conclusion, I think teaching is a noble and rewarding profession. I believe that with hard work and determination, I can become a great teacher who inspires many students. Teaching is not just a job; it is a calling.
